title: sempervivum tectorum subsp. atlanticum
intro: Sempervivum tectorum subsp. atlanticum, also known as Hens and Chicks or Atlantic Houseleek, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating burns, wounds, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sempervivum tectorum subsp. atlanticum include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a salve or ointment by infusing the leaves in a carrier oil, and it can also be consumed as a tea to leverage its medicinal properties.
